title: The Mirror and the Lamp: Romantic Theory and the Critical Tradition
excerpt: A landmark study that transformed the understanding of Romantic literary theory by distinguishing four critical orientations — mimetic, pragmatic, expressive, and objective — and tracing their development through the Romantic period.
keyIdeas: All critical theories can be classified by their orientation toward the work, the artist, the audience, or the universe
Romantic theory shifted from mimetic and pragmatic models to an expressive model centred on the poet's creative imagination
The mirror (neoclassical reflection) was replaced by the lamp (Romantic projection) as the master metaphor for art
Romantic critical innovations shaped the entire subsequent tradition of literary theory
keyTakeaways: Understand the four-coordinate system for classifying critical theories
Trace the historical shift from mimetic to expressive theories of art
Recognise Romantic criticism as a coherent intellectual movement with lasting influence
whoShouldRead: Students of Romantic literature and intellectual history
Scholars of literary theory and criticism
Anyone interested in the history of aesthetic ideas
whoShouldSkip: Readers seeking a general introduction to literary theory
Those primarily interested in post-Romantic or contemporary theory
